sprint: Screen 2 Preview Carousel Renders the Real Center Pane, Not a Static Thumbnail
task: User request: run the same center pane Studio's own Workspace uses inside Screen 2's Selected World Preview carousel, so an author can actually see what a World's page styles look like before starting. The carousel showed only a static (a Representation's authored thumbnail or the World's Hero Image/icon glyph); replaced with a real painted through the exact same SlideRenderer module the editor canvas uses, via a new synthetic-slide builder mirroring _finish()'s own theme-stamping logic (artworkTheme for an artwork-type World, theme for a story-type one) and a new render helper wrapping SlideRenderer.init()+render() with a graceful fallback to the old thumbnail on failure. A real, load-bearing bug was found and fixed while wiring this up: SlideRenderer.buildPayload() unconditionally resolves theme/artworkTheme off the GLOBAL ThemeEngine state, silently ignoring a slide's own theme/artworkTheme fields -- confirmed by comparing getCanvasSize(slide) (which DOES honour them) against the actual rendered canvas size before fixing it, not assumed. Fixed by overriding payload.theme/payload.artworkTheme with the synthetic slide's own values immediately before the one render() call this feature makes, with zero change to buildPayload() itself or any global state. Also restores the live editor canvas after painting the carousel (mirroring js/thumbnails.js's/js/pageOps.js's own established pattern), since Screen 2 can be reached from an already-open Workspace via the header's Home button. Verified via two new Playwright suites (general live-render + an aspect-differentiation suite proving Landscape/Portrait/Quote Representations render at their own genuinely correct canvas sizes -- the exact reproduction that caught the buildPayload bug) plus a visual screenshot, and full regression across the existing 33-script suite and both goldenBuild.js suites, all passing unchanged, zero console errors.
